questions.question: What does the Panchayat Elections (Amendment) Act, 2021, amend in the West Bengal Panchayat Act, 1973?
options: To increase the number of wards in gram panchayats
To allow for the election of panchayat presidents through direct voting
To provide reservation for SC/ST candidates in panchayat elections
To empower the state government to appoint panchayat presidents
questions.explanation: To provide reservation for SC/ST candidates in panchayat elections.

questions.question: Which article of the Constitution of India gives powers to the state governments to make laws for the welfare of SCs and STs?
questions.explanation: Article 46 provides for the welfare of Scheduled Castes.

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme launched by the Government of West Bengal to ensure the implementation of the Right to Education Act?
questions.explanation: Shiksha Sathi is the scheme to ensure the implementation of the Right to Education Act.

questions.question: Which of the following is a fundamental duty under the Constitution of India?
options: To preserve and protect the environment
To abide by the Constitution and respect its ideals
To participate in the defense of the country
questions.explanation: To abide by the Constitution and respect its ideals is a fundamental duty.

questions.question: What is the name of the committee constituted by the Government of West Bengal to examine the issues of the SCs and STs?
questions.explanation: The Bhattacharya Committee was constituted to examine the issues of the SCs and STs.

questions.question: Which of the following events marked the beginning of the Indian freedom struggle in Bengal?
questions.explanation: It was a highly divisive and emotive event that triggered widespread protests.

questions.question: Who was the famous Bengali revolutionary associated with the Jugantar movement?
questions.explanation: He was a key figure in the Jugantar movement.

questions.question: The famous 'Dacca Session' of the Indian National Congress was held in which year in Bengal?
questions.explanation: It was a significant session that witnessed intense debates and divisions.

questions.question: Which of the following was a major outcome of the Swadeshi Movement in Bengal?
options: Establishment of the Swadeshi textile mills
questions.explanation: It aimed to promote indigenous industries and reduce dependence on foreign goods.

questions.question: The famous 'Bengal National College' was founded in which year in Bengal?
questions.explanation: It was a pioneering educational institution that nurtured many freedom fighters.

questions.question: Tawang, a place in West Bengal, is situated near the border with which state?
questions.explanation: Tawang is near the Meghalaya border.

questions.question: The Sundarbans, the world's largest mangrove forest, is located in which of the following districts of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Sundarbans is located in South 24 Parganas district.

questions.question: What is the name of the highest peak in the Darjeeling Himalayan hill region of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Sandakphu is the highest peak in the Darjeeling Himalayan hill region.

questions.question: The Darjeeling Himalayan Railway, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, passes through which of the following mountain passes in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Darjeeling Himalayan Railway passes through the Jelep La mountain pass.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers originates in the Sikkim Himalayas and flows through West Bengal before emptying into the Bay of Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Torsa river originates in Sikkim Himalayas and flows through West Bengal.

questions.question: Which Indian state has recently implemented a new scheme to provide financial assistance to the beneficiaries under the Deen Dayal Upadhyaya Antyodaya Yojana (DAY-NULM) scheme?
questions.explanation: West Bengal has implemented the new scheme.

questions.question: What is the name of the newly launched river cruise service in West Bengal that connects the city of Kolkata to the Sunderbans?
questions.explanation: The Sunderban Queen is the newly launched river cruise service.

questions.question: Which of the following West Bengal government projects has been awarded the 'Best Urban Development Project' at the 2026 India Today Conclave?
questions.explanation: The Howrah Bridge Renovation project was awarded the 'Best Urban Development Project'.

questions.question: Which Indian state has recently partnered with the World Bank to implement a new project to improve the state's disaster management capabilities?
questions.explanation: West Bengal has partnered with the World Bank to implement the new project.

questions.question: Which of the following West Bengal government initiatives aims to promote entrepreneurship and skill development among the youth in the state?
questions.explanation: The Shubhaarambh initiative aims to promote entrepreneurship and skill development among the youth.

questions.question: Under which scheme is the 'Swasthya Sathi' health insurance card provided to the citizens of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It is a cashless health insurance scheme.

questions.question: Which of the following initiatives is a key component of the 'Sabuj Graam' scheme launched by the West Bengal government?
questions.explanation: It focuses on greening rural areas.

questions.question: The 'Kanyashree Prakalpa' scheme in West Bengal aims to empower and support which of the following sections of society?
questions.explanation: It focuses on education and marriage of girls.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key objective of the 'Siksha Sathi Yojana' launched by the West Bengal government?
options: Financial Assistance to Students from Low-Income Families
questions.explanation: It provides financial assistance to students from low-income families.

questions.question: The 'Mukhya Mantri Gram Dak Seva Yojana' is a scheme aimed at providing which of the following services to rural areas in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It focuses on postal services in rural areas.

questions.question: In a shop selling Bengali sweets, the cost price of a packet of rasgulla is ₹ 120. If the shopkeeper sells it at a 25% profit, then the selling price of the packet will be:
questions.explanation: The shopkeeper sells it at a 25% profit of ₹ 30.

questions.question: If a person invests ₹ 15,000 in shares of a West Bengal-based company and it appreciates by 8% in one year, then the amount of appreciation is:
questions.explanation: Amount of appreciation = 8% of ₹ 15,000.

questions.question: A shopkeeper in Kolkata buys a packet of tea for ₹ 250 and sells it for ₹ 300. His profit percentage is:
questions.explanation: Profit percentage = ((₹ 300 - ₹ 250) / ₹ 250) × 100.

questions.question: If the population of West Bengal increases by 15% in one year, and it was 91,000,000 in the previous year, then the present population is:
questions.explanation: Present population = 91,000,000 + (15% of 91,000,000).

questions.question: A person incurs a loss of 5% on selling an article for ₹ 1,200. The cost price of the article is:
questions.explanation: Loss percentage = 5% of cost price.

questions.question: A person can finish a piece of work in 30 days. He works for 5 days and then he hires 2 more people to complete the same work. How many days will it take for the three people to finish the work?
questions.explanation: Total work done in 5 days will be 1/6.

questions.question: Two pipes P and Q can fill a tank in 12 and 16 hours respectively. If both pipes operate simultaneously, how many hours will it take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Combined rate of filling the tank is 1/12 + 1/16 = 7/48.

questions.question: A worker can complete half of a job in 4 hours. Find the time required to complete the whole job.
questions.explanation: If 4 hours complete half of the work, then the whole work will take 8 hours.

questions.question: A pipe can fill a tank in 8 hours. If the pipe is closed for 2 hours, what will be the new time required to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Work done in 8 hours is 1. Time saved is 2 hours.

questions.question: A person can do a piece of work in 6 days, but due to illness, he was absent for 2 days. How many days will it take for him to complete the work now?
questions.explanation: Time lost is 2 days, so the new time required is 6 + 2 = 8 days.

questions.question: A train travels from Kolkata to Digha at an average speed of 120 km/h. If it takes 6 hours to cover a certain distance, how much time will it take to cover the same distance at an average speed of 90 km/h?
questions.explanation: Time taken is inversely proportional to speed.

questions.question: A car travels from Howrah to Siliguri at an average speed of 50 km/h. If it travels for 2 hours, what is the distance covered?
questions.explanation: Distance = Speed * Time.

questions.question: A cycle travels from Darjeeling to Kalimpong at an average speed of 20 km/h. If it takes 4 hours to cover a certain distance, what is the speed of the cycle if it covers the same distance in 3 hours?
questions.explanation: Speed is inversely proportional to time.

questions.question: A bus travels from Malda to Cooch Behar at an average speed of 60 km/h. If it travels for 3 hours, what is the distance covered if it travels at an average speed of 80 km/h for the same duration?
questions.explanation: Distance = Speed * Time.

questions.question: A ferry travels from Kakdwip to Raidighi at an average speed of 30 km/h. If it travels for 2.5 hours, what is the distance covered if it travels at an average speed of 40 km/h?
questions.explanation: Distance = Speed * Time.

questions.question: In the given code, 'মৃত্যু' is represented by a 2-digit number. What is the first digit of the number, if the second digit is '5'?
questions.explanation: The Bengali alphabet is used to represent numbers.

questions.question: If 'আমার বৈ' is coded as 'বাই আমা', then 'আমি দেখছি তোমাকে' would be coded as?
questions.explanation: The coded message is a reversal of words.

questions.question: A sentence is coded as 'মুন্না দেখেছি মেঘ' and 'মেঘ দেখেছি মুন্না'. What is the code for the sentence 'তোমার বাবা দেখেছে মুন্না'?
questions.explanation: The code is a reversal of words in the original sentence.

questions.question: What is the next number in the sequence ১, ৪, ৭, ১০, ১৩?
questions.explanation: The sequence is formed by adding ৩ to the previous number.

questions.question: If 'বড়' is represented by 'ছোট', 'ছোট' is represented by 'বড়', then 'খুব বড়' will be represented by?
questions.explanation: The representation is based on antonyms.

questions.question: A man's sister's husband's father is the grandfather of his wife's brother's son. How is the man related to his wife?
questions.explanation: Man is father-in-law of his wife.

questions.question: A grandmother's daughter's son's child is the child of a woman's son. What is the relationship of the child to the woman?
questions.explanation: Child is the grandchild of the woman.

questions.question: A man has three children: two sons and one daughter. His wife's sister also has three children: two sons and one daughter. Which of the following statements is true about the children of the two sisters?
options: At least one is a boy and one is a girl
questions.explanation: At least one child of the two sisters is a boy and one is a girl.

questions.question: Two men, A and B, are relatives. A's son is B's son's father. What is the relationship between A and B?
questions.explanation: A is the father and B is the son.

questions.question: A woman has two children, a son and a daughter. Her sister also has two children, a son and a daughter. Which of the following statements is true about the children of the two sisters?
options: At least one child of one of the sisters is a boy and one is a girl
All the children of both sisters are boys
All the children of both sisters are girls
questions.explanation: At least one child of one of the sisters is a boy and one is a girl.

questions.question: A person is walking on a road in Kolkata. He takes 15 steps towards North and then turns right and walks 10 steps. He then turns right and walks 5 steps. In which direction is he facing?
questions.explanation: Since he turned right twice, he is facing the initial direction

questions.question: A person is standing at a point P in Darjeeling. He walks 20 meters east from point P, then 15 meters north, then 5 meters east and finally 10 meters south. What is the direction of point P from his current position?
questions.explanation: Since he moved more in the northern direction, the current position is north of point P

questions.question: A person starts walking from a point A. He walks 20 meters towards West, then 15 meters towards South and finally 25 meters towards North-East. In which direction is he facing?
questions.explanation: After moving 20 meters West and 25 meters North-East, he is facing North-East

questions.question: A person starts walking from a point P. He walks 15 meters towards East and then 20 meters towards North. If he walks 15 meters in a direction which is at right angles to the direction in which he walked earlier, which of the following is the direction in which he walked 15 meters?
questions.explanation: Since he walked 15 meters earlier towards East, he walked 15 meters towards South

questions.question: A man is walking in a park with a circular shape. He is walking 8 km/hr in a direction which makes an angle of 60? with the direction of the boundary. In which direction is he walking?
questions.explanation: Since he is moving at an angle of 60? with the direction of boundary, he is moving in the North-West direction

questions.question: If 2, 5, 8, 11, 14 is a series, then the next number in the series will be?
questions.explanation: The difference between the consecutive terms is increasing by 1.

questions.question: Which of the following is the next letter in the series: K, N, Q, T?
questions.explanation: The series is formed by adding 3 to the alphabetical value of the previous letter.

questions.question: What is the missing number in the series: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, ______?
questions.explanation: The numbers are squares of consecutive natural numbers.

questions.question: Which of the following is the next number in the series: 3, 6, 12, 24, 48?
questions.explanation: The series is formed by doubling the previous number.

questions.question: What is the next letter in the series: A, D, G, J?
questions.explanation: The series is formed by adding 3 to the previous letter's alphabetical value.
